frameworks.name: finite element head modeling
frameworks.one_line: Individualized FEM predicts cortical electric field magnitude from anatomy.

core_question: How does scalp-to-cortex distance at left primary motor cortex relate to predicted electric field magnitude in non-invasive brain stimulation?
key_assumptions: Individualized MRI-derived anatomy is needed to predict cortical dose
Electric field at the cortical target indexes NIBS effective dose
manipulations: Inter-individual variation in scalp-to-cortex distance across N=50 adults
key_findings: Scalp-to-cortex distance showed substantial inter-individual variance
Predicted cortical current density scaled inversely with scalp-to-cortex distance
interpretations: Scalp-to-cortex distance should be a routinely reported covariate in NIBS studies
Scalp-to-cortex distance should inform personalized neurostimulation dosing
